summary: Small IF subsystem matching slice. The final loaded diff changes `src/melee/if/soundtest.c` by explicitly zero-initializing `un_803FA258`. The PR body says the slice was split from #2617 for easier review, scoped to `src/melee/if`, and local `git diff --check` passed. decomp-dev reported one new soundtest match and several soundtest item improvements. Review comments on `src/melee/if/textlib.c` objected to renaming variables and using defines in their place, but the final changed-file metadata only lists `soundtest.c`.

key_files.role: Final changed file; zero-initializes `un_803FA258`, with decomp-dev reporting one new match and several soundtest improvements.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: decomp-dev report for GALE01 lists `main/melee/if/soundtest` new match `un_80301BA8` and improvements in `un_802FFF2C`, `.data`, `un_80300AF4`, `un_80300B58`

key_files.role: PR body listed file and review comments discuss naming/define concerns, but final loaded changed-file metadata does not list it.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: only final changed file listed is `src/melee/if/soundtest.c`

what_changed.change: Added explicit aggregate zero initializer to `un_803FA258`.
decomp-dev reported the PR slice produced one new match for `main/melee/if/soundtest` item `un_80301BA8` and improvements in four soundtest items.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: Matched code +188 bytes; new match `un_80301BA8`; improvements `un_802FFF2C`, `.data`, `un_80300AF4`, `un_80300B58`

smart_moves.lesson: Splitting a larger matching branch by directory/subsystem can make matching improvements easier to review.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `Split from #2617 by directory/subsystem so the matching improvements are easier to review.`

smart_moves.lesson: A minimal initializer-only source change can affect soundtest matching outcomes, including text and data-related item scores.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: decomp-dev report lists one new match and soundtest improvements

decomp_lessons.lesson: For data/global cleanup in matching PRs, avoid replacing existing semantic variable names with address-style names plus defines unless there is explicit evidence supporting the rename.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: PsiLupan comment `Avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place.`
review_comments.md: hunk shows `devtext_drawlist`/`devtext_poolhead` being changed toward `un_804D6E18`

naming_conventions.convention: Do not rename established semantic globals such as `devtext_drawlist`, `devtext_poolhead`, or color globals to address-style names as a convenience layer with defines.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: first textlib hunk shows semantic DevText globals removed in favor of `un_804D6E18`
review_comments.md: second textlib hunk shows color globals removed in favor of `un_804DDC88`
review_comments.md: reviewer says to avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place

naming_conventions.convention: Keep one canonical evidence-backed global/data name rather than using identifier-to-identifier defines to mask a rename.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: two reviewer comments against renaming variables and using defines

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Explicitly zero-initialize a global aggregate when it matches the target object layout/codegen for the soundtest unit.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: decomp-dev report shows `main/melee/if/soundtest` match/improvement results
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice does not include author explanation proving why this initializer was required; treat as PR-specific evidence, not a global rule.

review_feedback.feedback: Avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place.
Same concern applied to color globals: avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: hunk around `GXColor white/red/green/blue` changed to `un_804DDC88`

follow_up_queries: GXColor white red green blue un_804DDC88 review

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, final changed_files count 1
human_pr_text.md: PR body scope, preliminary report, local checks, listed files
raw_diff.diff: complete final diff excerpt for soundtest.c
text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev report and review comments
notes: Final changed-file metadata and raw diff only show `src/melee/if/soundtest.c`; however, PR body and review comments reference `src/melee/if/textlib.c`, likely from an earlier revision or split context.
The PR body includes preliminary full-branch metrics, but it says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative once complete; the decomp-dev issue comment provides narrower PR-slice results.
No author explanation is loaded for why `un_803FA258 = { 0 }` was chosen, so the initializer tactic is PR-specific and should not be generalized without more evidence.
Review feedback is strong for the naming/define lesson, but the loaded final diff does not show the corresponding textlib changes as merged.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid define aliases for speculative global renames
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewer feedback in PR #2623 says to avoid renaming variables and using defines in their place. In the cited textlib hunks, existing names like `devtext_drawlist`, `devtext_poolhead`, and color globals were being replaced by address-style names, and the reviewer objected.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `un_803FA258 = { 0 }` into a global standard; the PR proves only that this exact soundtest initializer was part of a matching slice.
Do not claim textlib.c was a final changed file from this slice based solely on review comments; final changed-file metadata lists only soundtest.c.
Do not treat the preliminary full-branch metrics from the PR body as final PR-slice validation; the PR body explicitly defers to per-PR reporting.
